page: OVHcloud b2-30 is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Core Processor based on the Haswell architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The instance is configured with 8 dedicated physical cores, providing 8 vCPUs with a single thread per core. It includes 30.0 GB of system memory, translating to 3.75 GB per core, and is bundled with 200 GB of local SSD storage. A single complimentary public IPv4 address is included with the deployment.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance falling into the bottom 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the bottom 25%. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, as demonstrated by Geekbench multi-core, stress-ng multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth presents a similar tradeoff: while small-block cached reads achieve average speeds, large-block uncached writes are poor, placing in the bottom 10%.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced resource profile, the b2-30 offers stable performance for multi-threaded workloads that do not require high single-core clock speeds. It is suited for static web serving, Redis key-value stores, software compilation, and moderate database operations, all of which register average benchmark throughput. The instance provides a cost-efficient option for general-purpose workloads by offering dedicated physical cores and bundled SSD storage without the premium associated with high-frequency or specialized compute architectures.
description: The OVHcloud b2-30 is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 8 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Haswell architecture running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 30.0 GB of RAM and 200 GB of local SSD storage. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a balanced resource profile. Benchmark data indicates a significant performance tradeoff: single-core CPU performance and uncached memory write speeds are weak, falling into the bottom 10% to 25% of tested servers. Conversely, multi-core performance, software compilation, and database operations achieve average tier results. This makes the b2-30 a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for multi-threaded workloads such as static web serving, Redis key-value stores, and general-purpose applications that benefit from dedicated physical cores but do not demand high single-thread execution speeds.
og_description: OVHcloud b2-30 is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 8 dedicated Intel cores, 30 GB RAM, and 200 GB SSD storage, delivering balanced multi-core performance for standard workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud b2-30 is a general-purpose KVM virtual server with 8 dedicated Intel cores, 30 GB RAM, and 200 GB local SSD storage.
tagline: A general-purpose virtual server with dedicated physical cores and bundled SSD storage, optimized for balanced multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated physical Intel cores on x86_64 Haswell architecture
30.0 GB of system memory and 200 GB of local SSD storage
Average multi-core performance and software compilation capabilities
Weak single-core CPU performance and uncached memory write speeds
Suited for static web serving, Redis databases, and general-purpose applications
